Defines built-in RegExp object properties and identifiers for the JerryScript engine.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines macros and constants related to the built-in `RegExp` object in the ECMAScript language specification. It uses preprocessor directives to define and later undefine macros such as `OBJECT_ID`, `OBJECT_VALUE`, and `NUMBER_VALUE`, which are used to associate identifiers and properties with the `RegExp` object. The `OBJECT_ID` macro assigns an identifier to the `RegExp` object, while the `NUMBER_VALUE` macro sets the `length` property of the `RegExp` object to `2` with fixed attributes. The `OBJECT_VALUE` macro links the `RegExp` object to its prototype, `ECMA_BUILTIN_ID_REGEXP_PROTOTYPE`, also with fixed attributes. This file is part of a system that implements ECMAScript built-in objects, following the ECMA-262 standard.
